scenarios.scenario-1: New SNI policy becomes effective without breaking existing HTTPS portal traffic on 443.
scenarios.scenario-2: Upstream hook mismatch or routing regression is detected before public rollout.
scenarios.scenario-3: Client access using the raw base secret or an unissued SNI is rejected once per-SNI secret enforcement is enabled.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: 443 edge preserves both web and proxy behavior.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Policy application is observable and reversible on failure.
critical-invariants.invariant-3: Issued-domain whitelist and per-SNI secret enforcement move together so policy allowlisting is cryptographically meaningful.

side-effects.effect-1: Mutates runtime policy or routing state inside the proxy engine.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Do not log raw TLS private keys or full secret-bearing policy payloads.

verification-levels.module-local: Policy translation and hook selection tests.
verification-levels.wave-level: Edge-routing integration in local compose environment.
verification-levels.phase-level: Portal HTTPS and issued proxy domains coexist on live 443.

evidence.item: `validate_bridge_module.sh` and `validate_edge_contract.sh` prove current policy-hook wiring and shared-443 contract.
Upstream `_build/default/lib/mtproto_proxy/test/single_dc_SUITE.erl` documents `per_sni_secrets_on_case` and `per_sni_secrets_wrong_secret_case` as the target hardening behavior.
`validate_per_sni_secret_enforcement.sh` adds a local deterministic fake-TLS handshake probe proving derived-secret acceptance and raw-secret rejection when `per_sni_secrets` is enabled.

production-gates.gate-1: Connection using raw `PROXY_SECRET_HEX` fails while connection using an issued derived credential succeeds.
production-gates.gate-2: Repo-local verification demonstrates both derived-secret acceptance and raw-secret rejection before rollout.
